String.java

00033 module.  An independent module is a module which is not derived from

00036 obligated to do so.  If you do not wish to do so, delete this

00039 
00040 package java.lang;
00041 
00042 import java.lang.System;
00043 import java.io.UnsupportedEncodingException;
00044 import jelatine.VMPointer;
00045 
00071 public final class String
00072 {
00074     private VMPointer next;
00075 
00080     final char[] value;
00081 
00087     final int count;
00088 
00093     private int cachedHashCode;
00094 
00100     final int offset;
00101 
00106     public String()
00107     {
00108         value = "".value;
00109         offset = 0;
00110         count = 0;
00111     }
00112 
00120     public String(String str)
00121     {
00122         value = str.value;
00123         offset = str.offset;
00124         count = str.count;
00125         cachedHashCode = str.cachedHashCode;
00126     }
00127 
00135     public String(char[] data)
00136     {
00137         this(data, 0, data.length, false);
00138     }
00139 
00154     public String(char[] data, int offset, int count)
00155     {
00156         this(data, offset, count, false);
00157     }
00158 
00178     public String(byte[] data, int offset, int count, String encoding)
00179         throws UnsupportedEncodingException
00180     {
00181         if (offset < 0)
00182             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException("offset: " + offset);
00183 
00184         if (count < 0)
00185             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException("count: " + count);
00186 
00187         // equivalent to: offset + count < 0 || offset + count > data.length
00188         if (data.length - offset < count)
00189             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException("offset + count: "
00190                 + (offset + count));
00191 
00192         // Only ISO 8859-1 support is requested by the CLDC specification but
00193         // since ASCII is a subset of it we can support it as well
00194         if (!encoding.equals("ISO8859_1")
00195             && !encoding.equals("US_ASCII")
00196             && !encoding.equals("UTF-8"))
00197         {
00198             throw new UnsupportedEncodingException();
00199         }
00200 
00201         value = new char[count];
00202 
00203         for (int i = 0; i < count; i++)
00204             value[i] = (char) (data[i + offset] & 0xFF);
00205 
00206         this.offset = 0;
00207         this.count = count;
00208     }
00209 
00225     public String(byte[] data, String encoding)
00226         throws UnsupportedEncodingException
00227     {
00228         this(data, 0, data.length, encoding);
00229     }
00230 
00248     public String(byte[] data, int offset, int count)
00249     {
00250         if (offset < 0)
00251             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException("offset: " + offset);
00252 
00253         if (count < 0)
00254             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException("count: " + count);
00255 
00256         // equivalent to: offset + count < 0 || offset + count > data.length
00257         if (data.length - offset < count)
00258             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException("offset + count: "
00259                 + (offset + count));
00260 
00261         value = new char[count];
00262 
00263         for (int i = 0; i < count; i++)
00264             value[i] = (char) (data[i + offset] & 0xFF);
00265 
00266         this.offset = 0;
00267         this.count = count;
00268   }
00269 
00284     public String(byte[] data)
00285     {
00286         this(data, 0, data.length);
00287     }
00288 
00296     public String(StringBuffer buffer)
00297     {
00298         synchronized (buffer)
00299         {
00300             offset = 0;
00301             count = buffer.count;
00302 
00303             // Share unless buffer is 3/4 empty.
00304             if ((count << 2) < buffer.value.length)
00305             {
00306                 value = new char[count];
00307                 System.arraycopy(buffer.value, 0, value, 0, count);
00308             }
00309             else
00310             {
00311                 buffer.shared = true;
00312                 value = buffer.value;
00313             }
00314         }
00315     }
00316 
00327     String(char[] data, int offset, int count, boolean dont_copy)
00328     {
00329         if (offset < 0)
00330             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException("offset: " + offset);
00331 
00332         if (count < 0)
00333             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException("count: " + count);
00334 
00335         // equivalent to: offset + count < 0 || offset + count > data.length
00336         if (data.length - offset < count)
00337             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException("offset + count: "
00338                 + (offset + count));
00339 
00340         if (dont_copy)
00341         {
00342             value = data;
00343             this.offset = offset;
00344         }
00345         else
00346         {
00347             value = new char[count];
00348             System.arraycopy(data, offset, value, 0, count);
00349             this.offset = 0;
00350         }
00351 
00352         this.count = count;
00353     }
00354 
00360     public int length()
00361     {
00362         return count;
00363     }
00364 
00373     public char charAt(int index)
00374     {
00375         if (index < 0 || index >= count)
00376             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException();
00377 
00378         return value[offset + index];
00379     }
00380 
00396     public void getChars(int srcBegin, int srcEnd, char dst[], int dstBegin)
00397     {
00398         if (srcBegin < 0 || srcBegin > srcEnd || srcEnd > count)
00399             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException();
00400 
00401         System.arraycopy(value, srcBegin + offset, dst, dstBegin,
00402             srcEnd - srcBegin);
00403     }
00404 
00417     public byte[] getBytes(String encoding) throws UnsupportedEncodingException
00418     {
00419         if (!encoding.equals("ISO8859_1")
00420             && !encoding.equals("US_ASCII")
00421             && !encoding.equals("UTF-8"))
00422         {
00423             throw new UnsupportedEncodingException();
00424         }
00425 
00426         byte result[] = new byte[count];
00427 
00428         for (int i = 0; i < count; i++)
00429             result[i] = (byte) value[i + offset];
00430 
00431         return result;
00432     }
00433 
00443     public byte[] getBytes()
00444     {
00445         byte[] bytes = new byte[count];
00446 
00447         for(int i = 0; i < count; i++)
00448         {
00449             bytes[i] = (byte)
00450                 ((value[offset + i] <= 0xFF) ? value[offset + i] : '?');
00451         }
00452 
00453         return bytes;
00454     }
00455 
00465     public boolean equals(Object anObject)
00466     {
00467         if (! (anObject instanceof String))
00468             return false;
00469 
00470         String str2 = (String) anObject;
00471 
00472         if (count != str2.count)
00473             return false;
00474 
00475         if (value == str2.value && offset == str2.offset)
00476             return true;
00477 
00478         int i = count;
00479         int x = offset;
00480         int y = str2.offset;
00481 
00482         for (i = 0; i < count; i++)
00483         {
00484             if (value[x] != str2.value[y])
00485                 return false;
00486 
00487             x++;
00488             y++;
00489         }
00490 
00491         return true;
00492     }
00493 
00511     public boolean equalsIgnoreCase(String anotherString)
00512     {
00513         if (anotherString == null || count != anotherString.count)
00514             return false;
00515 
00516         int i = count;
00517         int x = offset;
00518         int y = anotherString.offset;
00519 
00520         while (--i >= 0)
00521         {
00522             char c1 = value[x++];
00523             char c2 = anotherString.value[y++];
00524             // Note that checking c1 != c2 is redundant, but avoids method calls.
00525 
00526             if (c1 != c2
00527                 && Character.toUpperCase(c1) != Character.toUpperCase(c2)
00528                 && Character.toLowerCase(c1) != Character.toLowerCase(c2))
00529             {
00530                 return false;
00531             }
00532       }
00533 
00534       return true;
00535     }
00536 
00551     public int compareTo(String anotherString)
00552     {
00553         int i = Math.min(count, anotherString.count);
00554         int x = offset;
00555         int y = anotherString.offset;
00556 
00557         while (--i >= 0)
00558         {
00559             int result = value[x++] - anotherString.value[y++];
00560 
00561             if (result != 0)
00562                 return result;
00563         }
00564 
00565         return count - anotherString.count;
00566     }
00567 
00585     public boolean regionMatches(boolean ignoreCase, int toffset, String other,
00586         int ooffset, int len)
00587     {
00588         if (toffset < 0 || ooffset < 0 || toffset + len > count
00589             || ooffset + len > other.count)
00590         {
00591             return false;
00592         }
00593 
00594         toffset += offset;
00595         ooffset += other.offset;
00596 
00597         while (--len >= 0)
00598         {
00599             char c1 = value[toffset++];
00600             char c2 = other.value[ooffset++];
00601             // Note that checking c1 != c2 is redundant when ignoreCase is true,
00602             // but it avoids method calls.
00603 
00604             if (c1 != c2
00605                 && (! ignoreCase
00606                     || (Character.toLowerCase(c1) != Character.toLowerCase(c2)
00607                         && (Character.toUpperCase(c1)
00608                             != Character.toUpperCase(c2)))))
00609             {
00610                 return false;
00611             }
00612         }
00613 
00614         return true;
00615     }
00616 
00629     public boolean startsWith(String prefix, int toffset)
00630     {
00631         return regionMatches(false, toffset, prefix, 0, prefix.count);
00632     }
00633 
00643     public boolean startsWith(String prefix)
00644     {
00645         return regionMatches(false, 0, prefix, 0, prefix.count);
00646     }
00647 
00657     public boolean endsWith(String suffix)
00658     {
00659         return regionMatches(false, count - suffix.count, suffix, 0, suffix.count);
00660     }
00661 
00669     public int hashCode()
00670     {
00671         if (cachedHashCode != 0)
00672             return cachedHashCode;
00673 
00674         // Compute the hash code using a local variable to be reentrant.
00675         int hashCode = 0;
00676         int limit = count + offset;
00677 
00678         for (int i = offset; i < limit; i++)
00679             hashCode = hashCode * 31 + value[i];
00680 
00681         return cachedHashCode = hashCode;
00682     }
00683 
00690     public int indexOf(int ch)
00691     {
00692         return indexOf(ch, 0);
00693     }
00694 
00705     public int indexOf(int ch, int fromIndex)
00706     {
00707         if ((char) ch != ch)
00708             return -1;
00709 
00710         if (fromIndex < 0)
00711             fromIndex = 0;
00712 
00713         int i = fromIndex + offset;
00714 
00715         for ( ; fromIndex < count; fromIndex++)
00716         {
00717             if (value[i++] == ch)
00718                 return fromIndex;
00719         }
00720 
00721         return -1;
00722     }
00723 
00730     public int lastIndexOf(int ch)
00731     {
00732         return lastIndexOf(ch, count - 1);
00733     }
00734 
00745     public int lastIndexOf(int ch, int fromIndex)
00746     {
00747         if ((char) ch != ch)
00748             return -1;
00749 
00750         if (fromIndex >= count)
00751             fromIndex = count - 1;
00752 
00753         int i = fromIndex + offset;
00754 
00755         for ( ; fromIndex >= 0; fromIndex--)
00756         {
00757             if (value[i--] == ch)
00758                 return fromIndex;
00759         }
00760 
00761         return -1;
00762     }
00763 
00771     public int indexOf(String str)
00772     {
00773         return indexOf(str, 0);
00774     }
00775 
00787     public int indexOf(String str, int fromIndex)
00788     {
00789         int i;
00790         boolean found;
00791 
00792         if (fromIndex < 0)
00793             fromIndex = 0;
00794 
00795         int limit = count - str.count;
00796 
00797         for ( ; fromIndex <= limit; fromIndex++)
00798         {
00799             found = true;
00800 
00801             for (i = 0; i < str.count; i++)
00802             {
00803                 if (str.value[i] != value[fromIndex + i])
00804                     found = false;
00805             }
00806 
00807             if (found)
00808                 return fromIndex;
00809         }
00810 
00811         return -1;
00812     }
00813 
00823     public String substring(int begin)
00824     {
00825         return substring(begin, count);
00826     }
00827 
00839     public String substring(int beginIndex, int endIndex)
00840     {
00841         if (beginIndex < 0 || endIndex > count || beginIndex > endIndex)
00842             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException();
00843 
00844         if (beginIndex == 0 && endIndex == count)
00845             return this;
00846 
00847         int len = endIndex - beginIndex;
00848         // Package constructor avoids an array copy.
00849         return new String(value, beginIndex + offset, len,
00850             (len << 2) >= value.length);
00851     }
00852 
00861     public String concat(String str)
00862     {
00863         if (str.count == 0)
00864             return this;
00865 
00866         if (count == 0)
00867             return str;
00868 
00869         char[] newStr = new char[count + str.count];
00870         System.arraycopy(value, offset, newStr, 0, count);
00871         System.arraycopy(str.value, str.offset, newStr, count, str.count);
00872         // Package constructor avoids an array copy.
00873         return new String(newStr, 0, newStr.length, true);
00874     }
00875 
00884     public String replace(char oldChar, char newChar)
00885     {
00886         if (oldChar == newChar)
00887             return this;
00888 
00889         int i = count;
00890         int x = offset - 1;
00891 
00892         while (--i >= 0)
00893         {
00894            if (value[++x] == oldChar)
00895                break;
00896         }
00897 
00898         if (i < 0)
00899             return this;
00900 
00901         char[] newStr = new char[value.length];
00902         System.arraycopy(value, 0, newStr, 0, value.length);
00903         newStr[x] = newChar;
00904 
00905         while (--i >= 0)
00906         {
00907             if (value[++x] == oldChar)
00908                 newStr[x] = newChar;
00909         }
00910 
00911         // Package constructor avoids an array copy.
00912         return new String(newStr, offset, count, true);
00913     }
00914 
00922     public String toLowerCase()
00923     {
00924         // First, see if the current string is already lower case.
00925         int i = count;
00926         int x = offset - 1;
00927 
00928         while (--i >= 0)
00929         {
00930             char ch = value[++x];
00931 
00932             if (ch != Character.toLowerCase(ch))
00933                 break;
00934         }
00935 
00936         if (i < 0)
00937             return this;
00938 
00939         // Now we perform the conversion. Fortunately, there are no multi-character
00940         // lowercase expansions in Unicode 3.0.0.
00941         char[] newStr = new char[value.length];
00942         System.arraycopy(value, 0, newStr, 0, value.length);
00943 
00944         do
00945         {
00946             char ch = value[x];
00947 
00948             // Hardcoded special case.
00949             newStr[x++] = Character.toLowerCase(ch);
00950         } while (--i >= 0);
00951 
00952         // Package constructor avoids an array copy.
00953         return new String(newStr, offset, count, true);
00954     }
00955 
00963     public String toUpperCase()
00964     {
00965         // First, see if the current string is already lower case.
00966         int i = count;
00967         int x = offset - 1;
00968 
00969         while (--i >= 0)
00970         {
00971             char ch = value[++x];
00972 
00973             if (ch != Character.toUpperCase(ch))
00974                 break;
00975         }
00976 
00977         if (i < 0)
00978             return this;
00979 
00980         // Now we perform the conversion. Fortunately, there are no multi-character
00981         // lowercase expansions in Unicode 3.0.0.
00982         char[] newStr = new char[value.length];
00983         System.arraycopy(value, 0, newStr, 0, value.length);
00984 
00985         do
00986         {
00987             char ch = value[x];
00988 
00989             // Hardcoded special case.
00990             newStr[x++] = Character.toUpperCase(ch);
00991         } while (--i >= 0);
00992 
00993         // Package constructor avoids an array copy.
00994         return new String(newStr, offset, count, true);
00995     }
00996 
01005     public String trim()
01006     {
01007         int limit = count + offset;
01008 
01009         if (count == 0 || (value[offset] > '\u0020'
01010             && value[limit - 1] > '\u0020'))
01011         {
01012             return this;
01013         }
01014 
01015         int begin = offset;
01016 
01017         do
01018         {
01019             if (begin == limit)
01020                 return "";
01021         } while (value[begin++] <= '\u0020');
01022 
01023         int end = limit;
01024         while (value[--end] <= '\u0020');
01025         return substring(begin - offset - 1, end - offset + 1);
01026     }
01027 
01033     public String toString()
01034     {
01035         return this;
01036     }
01037 
01044     public char[] toCharArray()
01045     {
01046         char[] copy = new char[count];
01047         System.arraycopy(value, offset, copy, 0, count);
01048         return copy;
01049     }
01050 
01059     public static String valueOf(Object obj)
01060     {
01061         return obj == null ? "null" : obj.toString();
01062     }
01063 
01074     public static String valueOf(char[] data)
01075     {
01076         return valueOf (data, 0, data.length);
01077     }
01078 
01095     public static String valueOf(char[] data, int offset, int count)
01096     {
01097         return new String(data, offset, count, false);
01098     }
01099 
01106     public static String valueOf(boolean b)
01107     {
01108         return b ? "true" : "false";
01109     }
01110 
01117     public static String valueOf(char c)
01118     {
01119         // Package constructor avoids an array copy.
01120         return new String(new char[] { c }, 0, 1, true);
01121     }
01122 
01130     public static String valueOf(int i)
01131     {
01132         // See Integer to understand why we call the two-arg variant.
01133         return Integer.toString(i, 10);
01134     }
01135 
01143     public static String valueOf(long l)
01144     {
01145         return Long.toString(l);
01146     }
01147 
01155     public static String valueOf(float f)
01156     {
01157         return Float.toString(f);
01158     }
01159 
01167     public static String valueOf(double d)
01168     {
01169         return Double.toString(d);
01170     }
01171 
01181     public native String intern();
01182 }
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:24 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
